seo.title: Context Window Management for AI Agents in Azure AI Agent Service Agents
seo.description: How to detect and fix context window management for ai agents in Azure AI Agent Service agents. Framework-specific detection, fixes, and Vex automation.

content.intro: In Azure AI Agent Service, Context Window Management issues arise when agents exceed token limits during multi-turn conversations, tool calls, and grounding data retrieval from Azure AI Search or Bing. Agents fail gracefully only if middleware validates token counts before submission to Azure OpenAI models, but native threading often concatenates history without truncation. This leads to 429 errors, incomplete responses, and security violations when sensitive data spills across truncated contexts.

howItManifests.scenario: Multi-turn agent threads with tool execution
howItManifests.symptom: AzureOpenAIChatCompletions returns 429 'token limit exceeded' after 10+ turns
howItManifests.impact: Agent halts mid-conversation, users experience dropped sessions

howItManifests.scenario: Grounding with Azure AI Search file retrieval
howItManifests.symptom: File content + metadata exceeds 128k token limit for gpt-4o
howItManifests.impact: Truncated grounding data causes hallucinated responses

howItManifests.scenario: Bing Search grounding in enterprise agents
howItManifests.symptom: Search snippets + conversation history overflow context window
howItManifests.impact: Outdated or irrelevant grounding due to forced truncation

howItManifests.symptom: Thread token count grows exponentially without summarization
howItManifests.impact: High Azure OpenAI costs and eventual hard failures

detection.name: Token Limit Pre-Check Middleware
detection.description: Intercepts Azure AI Agent Service requests and calculates total tokens before API call
detection.code:
from azure.ai.projects.client import ProjectsClient
from tiktoken import get_encoding

client = ProjectsClient.from_credential()
thread_id = 'your-thread-id'

# Get thread messages
messages = client.agents.get_thread_messages(thread_id)

# Calculate tokens
encoding = get_encoding('cl100k_base')
total_tokens = 0
for msg in messages:
    total_tokens += len(encoding.encode(msg.content))

model_limit = 128000  # gpt-4o
if total_tokens > model_limit * 0.9:
    print(f'⚠️ Token overflow detected: {total_tokens}/{model_limit}')

detection.name: Azure Monitor Token Overflow Alerts
detection.description: Sets up Azure Monitor alerts for 429 errors with token limit patterns
detection.code:
from azure.monitor.query import LogsQueryClient

query = """
AzureDiagnostics
| where Category == 'AzureOpenAIChatCompletions'
| where message_s contains 'token limit'
| where resultType_s == 'RequestFailure'
| summarize count() by bin(TimeGenerated, 5m)
| where count_ > 5
"""

client = LogsQueryClient.from_credential()
response = client.query_workspace(
    workspace_id='your-log-analytics-id',
    query=query,
    timespan='PT1H'
)

if response.tables:
    print('🚨 Token limit alerts triggered:', response.tables[0].rows)

detection.name: Thread Token Usage Dashboard
detection.description: TypeScript dashboard monitoring Azure AI Agent threads token consumption

fixes.name: Dynamic Context Summarization
fixes.description: Summarizes conversation history when approaching token limits
fixes.code:
from openai import AzureOpenAI
from tiktoken import get_encoding

client = AzureOpenAI(azure_endpoint='your-endpoint')
encoding = get_encoding('cl100k_base')

def summarize_context(messages, max_tokens=8000):
    context_tokens = sum(len(encoding.encode(msg['content'])) for msg in messages)
    
    if context_tokens > max_tokens:
        summary_prompt = 'Summarize this conversation preserving key facts:'
        summary_response = client.chat.completions.create(
            model='gpt-4o-mini',
            messages=[{'role': 'user', 'content': summary_prompt + str(messages)}]
        )
        return [{'role': 'system', 'content': 'Conversation summary: ' + summary_response.choices[0].message.content}]
    return messages[-10:]  # Keep recent messages

# Use in agent loop
thread_messages = summarize_context(get_thread_messages())

fixes.name: Token-Budgeted Tool Calls
fixes.description: Limits tool response size before adding to context
fixes.code:
import json

def validate_tool_result(tool_result: dict, token_budget: int = 4000) -> str:
    encoding = get_encoding('cl100k_base')
    
    content = json.dumps(tool_result)
    tokens = len(encoding.encode(content))
    
    if tokens > token_budget:
        # Truncate intelligently
        return json.dumps({
            'content': content[:token_budget*4],
            'truncated': True,
            'original_tokens': tokens
        })
    return content

# In Azure Agent Service tool handler
result = validate_tool_result(tool_output)
agent_memory.append({'role': 'tool', 'content': result})

fixes.description: TypeScript implementation of fixed-size message buffer

vexAutomation.heading: Automating Context Window Management for AI Agents Detection with Vex
vexAutomation.description:
Vex integrates as middleware in the Azure AI Agent Service processing pipeline, automatically intercepting all agent threads and tool calls to monitor token consumption in real-time. Using the Vex SDK (pip install vex-sdk), it calculates context window usage across conversation history, grounding data, and tool outputs, flagging threads exceeding 90% of model limits. Vex provides automated remediation through dynamic summarization, rolling window truncation, and policy-based content filtering, ensuring enterprise compliance while maintaining agent performance.

The platform generates comprehensive dashboards showing token utilization trends per agent, thread, and model, with one-click remediation workflows. Vex's Azure-native integration leverages Azure Monitor and Application Insights for historical analysis, predicting token overflow before it causes 429 errors. Teams can enforce organization-wide context window policies with zero-code configuration.
vexAutomation.code:
from vex_sdk import VexAgentMonitor
from azure.ai.projects.client import ProjectsClient

# Install: pip install azure-ai-projects vex-sdk

vex_monitor = VexAgentMonitor(
    azure_tenant_id='your-tenant',
    subscription_id='your-sub',
    resource_group='rg-agents'
)

@vex_monitor.context_window_guard(model='gpt-4o', threshold=0.9)
def process_agent_thread(thread_id: str):
    client = ProjectsClient.from_credential()
    messages = client.agents.get_thread_messages(thread_id)
    
    # Vex auto-summarizes if over threshold
    optimized_context = vex_monitor.optimize_context(messages)
    
    response = client.agents.create_message(
        thread_id=thread_id,
        message={'role': 'user', 'content': 'continue'}
    )
    return response

# Vex auto-instruments all threads
vex_monitor.start_monitoring(project_name='enterprise-agents')

# Detection + auto-fix reports
print(vex_monitor.get_token_violations_report())
